file.content:
# Fixture: network-graph
## Task
Small network with ~30 nodes colored by community. Force-directed or circular layout.
## Inputs
- data.json: {nodes[{id, community}], edges[{source,target}], communities}
## Outputs
- figure.pdf, figure.png; caption.tex; gen_figure.py
## Hints
- networkx + matplotlib is fine.
- Avoid hairball: tune edge alpha and node size.

content.thinking: Now I have the data. Let me create the gen_figure.py script. The network has 30 nodes (0-29) with 4 communities (0-3), and edges connecting them. I'll use networkx with a spring layout (force-directed) and color nodes by community.

content.text: Now I'll write the generation script and run it.

input.content:
import json
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
community_map = {n["id"]: n["community"] for n in data["nodes"]}
for n in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(n["id"], community=n["community"])
for e in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(e["source"], e["target"])

n_communities = data["communities"]
palette = ["#E63946", "#457B9D", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A"]
node_colors = [palette[community_map[n]] for n in G.nodes()]

np.random.seed(42)
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes())), iterations=100)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
ax.set_aspect("equal")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

legend_handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[c], label=f"Community {c}")
    for c in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_handles, loc="upper left", framealpha=0.85,
          fontsize=8, title="Community", title_fontsize=8)

ax.set_title("Force-directed network graph (30 nodes, 4 communities)", fontsize=10, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

import json
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
community_map = {n["id"]: n["community"] for n in data["nodes"]}
for n in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(n["id"], community=n["community"])
for e in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(e["source"], e["target"])

n_communities = data["communities"]
palette = ["#E63946", "#457B9D", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A"]
node_colors = [palette[community_map[n]] for n in G.nodes()]

np.random.seed(42)
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes())), iterations=100)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
ax.set_aspect("equal")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

legend_handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[c], label=f"Community {c}")
    for c in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_handles, loc="upper left", framealpha=0.85,
          fontsize=8, title="Community", title_fontsize=8)

ax.set_title("Force-directed network graph (30 nodes, 4 communities)", fontsize=10, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

input.content: \caption{Force-directed layout of a 30-node network partitioned into four communities (colours). Nodes are positioned by a spring-embedding algorithm (NetworkX, seed 42); edge transparency ($\alpha=0.25$) reduces visual clutter. Cross-community edges reveal the ring-like backbone connecting the four groups.}
